title: Media releases
content:
#### Watch out for scammers this tax season
- 11 June 2026 Business Changes coming to how taxpayer ruling applications are submitted Inland Revenue (IR) is changing how customers submit taxpayer ruling applications, bringing the application process more in line with other IR processes.
- 16 March 2026 Nonpayment of PAYE a serious offence Inland Revenue (IR) is warning employers that making deductions from an employee’s salary or wages, and not passing them on to IR, is a serious offence that can lead to prison time.
- 29 April 2026 Mother and daughter sentenced for tax evasion A mother and daughter were sentenced to home detention and community work for tax evasion totalling nearly $2 million
- 20 April 2026 International Tax Crypto investors urged to get tax compliant Inland Revenue (IR) is reminding investors of crypto-assets that they need to get tax compliant now, so they don’t end up with an expensive surprise down the line.
- 02 April 2026 Two-step verification helps contain cyber attack Two-step verification (2SV) has helped contain a concerted, automated effort to access myIR accounts.
Inland Revenue has collected more than $207 million in repayments since July last year from student loan borrowers living overseas – a 43% increase on the same period the previous year.
A now bankrupt former Wellington tax agent was sentenced to home detention when he appeared in court this week on four representative charges related to COVID support offending.
